text:
The web search found a paper titled "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities," but it is authored by Brian Chen, David R. Karger, and Aleksander Madry (2016), not by Robert Kleinberg and Sergei M. Krakovski in SODA 2005. The authors are completely different, and the year and venue do not match. This is a classic case of a "grafted" reference — real title, fabricated authors, wrong venue and year.

VERDICT: LIKELY
EXPLANATION: A paper titled "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities" exists, but it is authored by Brian Chen, David R. Karger, and Aleksander Madry (2016), not by Robert Kleinberg and Sergei M. Krakovski in SODA 2005. The cited authors are completely wrong, and no SODA 2005 paper with this title was found in any database or via web search.
LINK: https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Multiple-Choice-Prophet-Inequalities-Chen-Karger/242b08107d5582fd1ccdf9975492c12ba383a3bd
